name: standards-refresh.sh refuses to write a source's YAML file when any rule violates the no-clause-silently-dropped fidelity contract
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r18-$$; mkdir -p $OUT/out && cat > $OUT/s.html <<'HTML'

Doc

Rule one

prose one.

HTML cat > $OUT/reg.yaml < $OUT/dropping-drafter.sh <<'SH' #!/usr/bin/env bash echo '{"clauses_total":2,"clauses_covered":1,"clauses_fallback":0,"clauses_unenforceable":0,"needs_operator_signoff":false,"no_clause_dropped":false}' exit 1 SH cd $OUT && CTP_STAGE5_DRAFT_CMD=$OUT/dropping-drafter.sh bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/standards-refresh.sh" --registry $OUT/reg.yaml --force --now 2026-07-11T00:00:00Z --out-dir $OUT/out 2>e.err; rc=$?; [ $rc -eq 1 ] && [ ! -f $OUT/out/european-union/r18-src.yaml ] && grep -q 'FIDELITY-VIOLATION' e.err && grep -q 'no_clause_dropped=false' e.err && echo clause-drop-refuses-write 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
